summary: This source is a brief introductory overview from the Stanford Encyclopedia of Philosophy regarding Mary Wollstonecraft. It outlines her role as an Enlightenment moral and political philosopher who analyzed the condition of women and argued for societal changes necessary for both sexes to achieve virtue and happiness.

principles.statement: Society must undergo fundamental change for both men and women to be virtuous in the private and public spheres.
principles.rationale: Achieving virtue in both private and public life is the necessary foundation for securing a measure of human happiness.

anti_patterns.description: Allowing civilization and human relations to be governed by acquisitiveness and consumption.
anti_patterns.why_it_fails: It distorts human relations and prevents the cultivation of true virtue and happiness in society.
